title: platform-user — Eloquent observers
description: Two observers. `PlatformUserObserver` gates status transitions via a hand-rolled state machine, fires the lifecycle events, invalidates the on-call resolver cache, and enforces the MFA-on-hire gate. `PlatformProfileObserver` handles media pruning (avatar removal when avatar_url is nulled or replaced) + fires PlatformProfileUpdated with the exact set of changed fields.

handles: creating → populate ULID prefix `plu_`

creating → refuse when the referenced Identity has NULL mfa_secret_encrypted AND config('platform-user.mfa.require_on_hire')=true (default). Throws `IDENTITY_MFA_REQUIRED`. This is the enrolment gate for the platform_admin guard — tenant Users can login without MFA, platform staff cannot.
creating → refuse when another platform_users row already references the same identity_id (defensive check beyond the UNIQUE constraint — preferred error shape).
creating → refuse when manager_id resolves to a row that is NOT `active` (cannot report to a suspended/offboarded manager).

creating → set status=`pending` as the default; explicit callers pass `active` via HirePlatformUserInput when hiring an already-onboarded employee (rare — usually onboarding checklist completion promotes pending→active).
creating → stamp created_by from the request-context actor.
created → fire PlatformUserHired event (afterCommit).

updating (status transition attempted) → validate transition against the state machine: pending→active, active→suspended, suspended→active, {pending|active|suspended}→offboarded. Any other transition throws `EmploymentLifecycleTransitionRefused`.
updating (status→active from pending) → fire PlatformUserActivated(previous_status=pending) on afterCommit.
updating (status→active from suspended) → fire PlatformUserActivated(previous_status=suspended) AND PlatformUserRestored on afterCommit — both events dispatch so listeners keyed on either name react.

updating (status→suspended) → refuse when the row has active impersonation sessions (access::PlatformImpersonationSession where ended_at IS NULL) — must terminate them first via the delegation module.
updating (status→suspended) → refuse when reason is missing or < 20 chars. Fire PlatformUserSuspended on afterCommit.
updating (status→offboarded) → refuse when the row has direct reports (manager_id IS <this.id> on any active/suspended platform_users row) — must reassign first.
updating (status→offboarded) → populate offboarded_at = NOW() (or effective_at when future-dated); soft-delete the row (deleted_at = offboarded_at) so the retention hold timer starts.
updating (status→offboarded) → fire PlatformUserOffboarded on afterCommit, which dispatches RevokePlatformUserAccessJob to notifications-critical.
updating (department mutated) → fire PlatformUserDepartmentChanged on afterCommit.
updating (manager_id mutated) → fire PlatformUserManagerReassigned on afterCommit; refuse cycles (cannot reassign to a manager whose chain of managers eventually points back to this row).
updating (on_call_rotation_key mutated) → invalidate the on-call resolver cache tag `platform-user:on-call:current` so the next resolution re-queries.
deleted → no-op (soft delete is orchestrated in the offboard flow; unsoftDelete is a manual super_admin override that fires PlatformUserRestored).
forceDeleted → no-op (hard delete is orchestrated by PurgeOffboardedPlatformUsersJob, which also purges the PlatformProfile in the same transaction).
creating → populate ULID prefix `plp_`
creating → stamp created_by from the request-context actor
creating → validate phone_e164 via `valid_e164_phone` rule (defensive — the DTO validation should have caught this)
creating → validate timezone via `valid_iana_timezone` rule (defensive)

updating (avatar_url mutated to a different value) → schedule PruneOldAvatarJob to hard-delete the previous S3 object (via the platform-media transport) 24h after the change (allows accidental-rollback within the day)
updating (avatar_url set to null from a previous value) → schedule PruneOldAvatarJob immediately (explicit avatar removal signals intent).

updated → fire PlatformProfileUpdated on afterCommit with `changed_fields` populated from the dirty attributes set (subset of first_name, last_name, display_name, avatar_url, phone_e164, slack_handle, github_handle, timezone).

deleting → refuse when the parent PlatformUser row still exists AND is NOT hard-deleted (PlatformProfile is 1:1 with PlatformUser — orphaning is a bug). This defends against direct PlatformProfile deletes; the correct path is hard-deleting PlatformUser which cascades to PlatformProfile via PurgeOffboardedPlatformUsersJob's transaction.
